commit.message:
Support RESP3 with hiredis-py parser (#3648)

* Add minimalistic implementation for Push notifications parsing with hiredis-py

* Fixing linters(almost all errors). Fixing hiredis parser for invalidation messages when using connection pool.

* Fixing last linter errors. Fixes needed for pubsub message handling with hiredis.

* Fix handle_pubsub_push_response

* Update redis/utils.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix formatting

* Some good sleep solves a lot of problems. Isn't it?

* Enable verbose output from pytest to check why tests are skipped

* Revert

* Bump hiredis version to 3.2.0

* Bump hiredis-py version in CI

---------

repository.name: agenttrace
repository.description: Open-source observability platform for AI coding agents. LLM traces, evaluations, prompt management, and dataset experiments with Langfuse feature parity.

commit.message:
feat(api): add automated runbook engine

Implement incident runbook engine with YAML-defined playbooks supporting
conditional steps, threshold-based triggers, and automated remediation
actions (scale, rollback, alert, block). Includes execution history
tracking and dry-run mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: agenttrace
repository.description: Open-source observability platform for AI coding agents. LLM traces, evaluations, prompt management, and dataset experiments with Langfuse feature parity.

commit.message:
feat(api): add prompt version impact analysis

Implement prompt impact analysis comparing versions across quality,
latency, cost, and safety dimensions with statistical significance
testing (chi-square, t-test, Mann-Whitney U). Generates keep/revert/
monitor recommendations based on configurable thresholds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Azure Gov deployment — RAI policy, workload profiles, log analytics

- Remove raiPolicyName from OpenAI deployments (not available in Azure Gov)
- Add workload profiles (Consumption) to Container Apps environment (required in Gov)
- Add logAnalyticsSharedKey output from monitoring, pass to container-apps
- Fix deploy.ps1 output capture to use separate az deployment group show
- Simplify setup-app-registration.ps1 to use az ad app create flags directly

Deployment verified: all resources created in rg-AgentOpsHub-dev (usgovarizona)
Container Apps running: web, api, mcp-gateway

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: smart-ops-hub
repository.description: AI-enabled operations platform with multi-agent chat workspace, deployed to Azure Government. .NET 9, Blazor Server, MCP servers, Azure Container Apps.

* Release v1.1.0 — docs and version updates

Prepare and finalize the v1.1.0 release: mark README as latest stable release and update changelog pointers; add RELEASE_GITHUB_1.1.0.md; expand and update RELEASE_NOTES_1.1.0.md (release date, features, bug fixes, ADRs, migration notes and deprecation table). Update many source and web asset headers from v1.1.0-beta to v1.1.0, refresh data/version.hash, and apply related wording/UX clarifications (PS/gateway mode, WebUI persistence, API v2 migration notes, diagnostic logging, serial buffer/overflow handling).

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* Bump OTGW firmware build to 2642

Increment OTGW firmware build to 2642 and update git hash to "0e35632". Refresh version date/time to 02-03-2026 21:19:50 and update related semver macros and version.hash so firmware metadata remains consistent.

* CI: update version.h

* Removing the beta label from the main stable version

* CI: update version.h

* Prepare v1.2.0 release: update docs & version

Finalize v1.2.0 release by removing beta/pre-release wording and updating release metadata. README and RELEASE_NOTES updated to reflect stable v1.2.0 (condensed prior sections and fixed wording/typos), RELEASE_GITHUB_1.2.0.md added for the GitHub release body, and docs/RELEASE_PROCESS.md added with a pre-release checklist and steps. Update src/OTGW-firmware/version.h to remove the -beta pre-release suffix and set the stable semver strings.

* Update release workflow to include .ino.bin files

Replace the artifact glob `build/*.bin` with `build/*.ino.bin` in the release workflow. This ensures the release job uploads Arduino-style .ino.bin artifacts (instead of a generic .bin pattern), so the correct build outputs are attached to releases.

* CI: update version.h

* Release v1.2.0

Update project version from v1.2.0-beta to v1.2.0 across firmware sources and web assets. Bump build metadata and git hash in version.h (build 2645, githash a0badbe), update version.timestamp and version.hash file. This prepares the codebase and bundled UI files for the 1.2.0 release.

* CI: update version.h

* CI: update version.h

* docs: re-add development branch warning banner to README

commit.message:
docs: fix uneven card sizes on homepage

Root cause: Starlight's .sl-markdown-content applies margin-top to
sibling elements (lobotomized owl selector), causing grid items 2-N
to shift down. Also items weren't stretching to fill grid cells.

Fix: Added margin: 0 !important and height: 100% to .workflow-step
and .feature-card. Shortened Plan and Sketch Planning descriptions
to avoid extra text wrapping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove unused ghenv function from zshrc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address Humanizer migration findings — i64 support, MCP improvements

Compiler/Language fixes:
- Fix integer overflow data corruption: widen IntLiteralNode and
  BoundIntLiteral from int to long, preventing silent truncation of
  C# long literals during C#→Calor conversion
- Lexer: add long.TryParse fallback for values outside int range
- Lexer: support numeric separators (1_000_000) by consuming underscores
- Lexer: fix hex literal truncation — store as long when > int.MaxValue
- CSharpEmitter: add L suffix for values outside int range
- RoslynSyntaxVisitor: remove (int)longVal cast that caused data corruption

MCP improvements:
- McpServer: set Console.InputEncoding/OutputEncoding to UTF-8 in
  CreateStdio() to fix § character corruption on Windows
- BatchConvertTool: return structured validation errors with line,
  column, category, and suggestion fields instead of flat strings
- CompileTool: add errorCategories summary to batch compilation output
  showing error counts grouped by error code
- DiagnoseTool: add 5 new error-suggestion patterns for common
  converter output issues (§NEW{object}, integer overflow, generic
  type params, IF ID mismatch)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Calor - Coding Agent Language for Optimized Reasoning

Add USB web interface transport (no dev mode required) (#55)

* Initial plan

* Add USB web interface client implementation and tests

Co-authored-by: SamMorrowDrums <4811358+SamMorrowDrums@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update documentation for USB web interface

Co-authored-by: SamMorrowDrums <4811358+SamMorrowDrums@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(usb-web): use API fileType, extract raw files from rmdoc archive

Based on live device testing, fix USB web client to match actual API:
- Capture fileType from API response instead of guessing from name
- Extract raw PDF/EPUB from .rmdoc archive (they're embedded)
- Add file_type, bookmarked, current_page fields to Document
- Position USB Web as recommended default connection mode in README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename --usb-web flag to --usb, clean up docs

Rename CLI flag from --usb-web to --usb for simplicity.
Remove unnecessary 'no developer mode' messaging from USB docs —
describe what USB mode is, not what it isn't.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase download timeout to 120s, fix duplicate docs line

Large documents (e.g. 29MB epub) can take >10s over USB. Use a
separate 120s timeout for download requests while keeping 10s for
metadata/listing calls. Also fix broken download_raw_file fallback
and duplicate bullet point in usb-web-setup.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add v5 .rm file renderer as fallback for older notebooks

rmc only supports v6+ format. Older notebooks use v5 (.lines format)
which caused render failures. Add a built-in v5 parser that reads the
binary stroke data and generates SVG directly, used as automatic
fallback when rmc fails. Also fix misleading error messages that
suggested installing already-required dependencies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add notebook cell support for isort (#565)

* Add notebook cell support for isort LSP server

- Add notebook document sync options for jupyter-notebook and interactive
- Add LSP handlers for notebook did_open, did_change, did_save, did_close
- Remove notebook cell guard in server-less code action provider
- Use uri.toString() instead of fsPath for code action data

* Fix review issues in notebook support

- Add missing PEP 8 blank lines between top-level functions in lsp_server.py
- Restore notebook cell guard in server-less mode code action provider
- Revert unrelated package-lock.json peer changes
- Fix prettier formatting (catch {}, empty arrow functions)

* Handle cell structure changes in notebook_did_change

Lint newly added cells and clear diagnostics for removed cells,
not just cells with text content changes.

* Fix _get_document_path docstring example

The function resolves notebook cell URIs to the notebook's .ipynb
path (stripping the cell fragment), not to a .py path.

* Add tests for notebook document LSP handlers

- Add notebook notification methods to LspSession test client
- Add tests for notebookDocument/didOpen, didChange, didSave, didClose
- Test cell structure changes (adding new cells with unsorted imports)
- Test diagnostics are cleared on notebook close

* Add notebook types to lsprotocol mock in test_get_cwd

The mock lsprotocol.types module was missing notebook-related
constants and types needed by the module-level NOTEBOOK_SYNC_OPTIONS
in lsp_server.py, causing import failures in CI.

* Run black on lsp_server.py

* Run black on test_notebook.py

* Filter out non-code cells in notebook_did_open

Use cell.kind check to skip markdown cells, matching the pattern
used in notebook_did_save.

* Use uri.toString() in notebook cell warning log

Consistent with the other .fsPath to .toString() changes in this
file — fsPath loses scheme information for non-file URIs.

* Support notebook cells in server-less mode

Add getDocumentPath helper to runner.ts that resolves notebook cell
URIs to filesystem paths (mirroring _get_document_path in Python).
Remove the isNotebookCell guard in sortImports.ts since the runner
can now handle notebook URIs.

* Fix _make_notebook_uri to use file: scheme

The notebook document URI should use the file: scheme (it represents
the .ipynb file on disk). Only individual cell URIs use the
vscode-notebook-cell: scheme.

* Remove is_python guard and add incomplete code tests

Remove is_python, strip_magic_commands, and MAGIC_COMMAND_REGEX from
lsp_server.py. The is_python check used ast.parse which rejected
valid-but-incomplete Python code, preventing organize imports from
working on files being actively edited.

Closes #243

Also removes now-unused ast and re imports.

Adds tests for both regular documents and notebook cells with
incomplete Python code (missing function body) to verify isort
correctly sorts imports without the is_python guard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Import sorting for python using the isort library.

commit.message:
Add Labs 049-050: Foundry IQ Agent Tracing & Multi-Agent Observability

Lab 049: Foundry IQ — Agent Tracing with OpenTelemetry (L300, ~75min)
- OpenTelemetry instrumentation for AI agents with GenAI semantic conventions
- 10 sample traces (sample_traces.csv) for offline analysis
- traced_agent.py: 5 TODOs (TracerProvider, spans, token attrs, root span, errors)
- broken_tracing.py: 3 bugs (OK-only avg, wrong token rates, error/error denominator)
- SVG: foundry-iq-tracing.svg (tracing architecture diagram)
- KC answers: avg latency=3150ms, total tokens=3255, highest=t006 at 8500ms

Lab 050: Multi-Agent Observability with GenAI Conventions (L400, ~120min)
- 46 spans across 5 traces showing Router→Specialist→Reviewer pipeline
- Includes retry pattern (trace A005: failed review, re-invoked specialist)
- multi_agent_spans.csv: hierarchical spans with parent-child relationships
- dashboard_builder.py: 5 TODOs (load, agent spans, LLM usage, tools, quality)
- broken_conventions.py: 3 bugs (span_name vs agent_name, count all vs agents, missing output_tokens)
- SVG: multi-agent-tracing.svg (trace hierarchy + GenAI conventions reference)
- KC answers: 16 agent spans, 7 unique agents, A002 most tools (3), avg quality=0.790

Both labs work offline (no Azure required) with provided datasets.
Labs 049+050 marked as implemented in proposed-labs.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite CONTRIBUTING.md to explain the private-to-public workflow:

Update README.md contributing section to match.

The previous fork-based workflow and stale azure-ai-foundry/doc-samples

commit.message:
feat: add 5 additive schema fields for ForgeTerrain high-priority features

Add optional fields to terrain_pack.v1 and resolved_map.v1 schemas:

- terrain_pack.v1: materialProperties (per-material gameplay metadata)
- resolved_map.v1: collisionGrid (derived collision/walkability grid)
- resolved_map.v1: variantWeights (per-material variant weight table)
- resolved_map.v1: cellClassification (opt-in per-cell classification provenance)
- resolved_map.v1: layerManifest (structured layer metadata)

All fields are optional and additive — no breaking changes.

Closes #13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ForgeContracts
